_note: Round-7 follow-up #4: when a component has multiple `<slot name="x">` sites for the same name (or multiple bare `<slot>` sites both defaulting to `'default'`), upstream's `slot.ts:279` stores them in a Map keyed by slot name — `set(slotName, attrs)` makes the LAST site win. Native pre-fix pushed every SlotDef in walk order; the slots literal then carried duplicate keys (`{ 'header': { value: 1 }, 'header': { value: 'late' } }`). At the user-facing diagnostic level this happens to be invisible today: the duplicate-key TS1117 fires at a position INSIDE the synth render-fn body, which our source-map filter drops as scaffolding. But it diverges from upstream's emit shape and would surface the moment the slot-let resolution pipeline lands on the strict slot path. Post-fix native dedups at the analyze layer with later-wins semantics: only one `'header'` entry survives, carrying the LAST occurrence's attrs (`{ value: 'late' }`). This fixture's role is the no-error baseline that pins the post-fix behaviour against future regressions.
